lede: A look at August 6 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Takhmina Ikromova and Leandro Amaral, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: 2015 Abha mosque bombing
events.desc: A suicide bomb attack kills at least 15 people at a mosque in the Saudi city of Abha.

events.title: 2010 Ladakh floods
events.desc: Flash floods across a large part of Jammu and Kashmir, India, damages 71 towns and kills at least 255 people.

events.title: Korean Air Flight 801
events.desc: Korean Air Flight 801 crashed at Nimitz Hill, Guam, killing 229 of the 254 people on board.

events.title: Takako Doi
events.desc: Takako Doi, chair of the Social Democratic Party, becomes Japan's first female speaker of the House of Representatives.

events.title: Lyndon B. Johnson
events.desc: US President Lyndon B. Johnson signs the Voting Rights Act of 1965 into law.

After going bankrupt in 1955, the American broadcaster DuMont Television Network makes its final broadcast, a boxing match from St. Nicholas Arena in New York in the Boxing from St. Nicholas Arena series.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Hiroshima, Japan is devastated when the atomic bomb "Little Boy" is dropped by the United States B-29 Enola Gay. Around 70,000 people are killed instantly, and some tens of thousands die in subsequent years from burns and radiation poisoning.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: U-boat campaign: Two days after the United Kingdom had declared war on Germany over the German invasion of Belgium, ten German U-boats leave their base in Heligoland to attack Royal Navy warships in the North Sea.

events.title: American Civil War
events.desc: American Civil War: The Confederate ironclad CSS Arkansas is scuttled on the Mississippi River after suffering catastrophic engine failure near Baton Rouge, Louisiana.

events.title: Constitution of the United States
events.desc: Sixty proof sheets of the Constitution of the United States are delivered to the Constitutional Convention in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania.

births.name: Kevin Mitnick
births.line: American computer security consultant, author, and convicted hacker (died 2023)

births.name: Cecil Howard Green
births.line: English-American geophysicist and businessman, co-founded Texas Instruments (died 2003)

deaths.text: Marvin Hamlisch, American pianist, composer, and conductor
Feodor Lynen, German biochemist and academic, Nobel Prize laureate
Lionel Cranfield, 1st Earl of Middlesex, English merchant and politician

observances.text: Christian holidays and observances Transfiguration of Jesus
Christian holidays and observances Anna Maria Rubatto
Christian holidays and observances Justus and Pastor
Christian holidays and observances August 6 (Eastern Orthodox liturgics)
Sheikh Zayed bin Sultan Al Nahyan's Accession Day. (United Arab Emirates)
Independence Day (Bolivia), celebrates the independence of Bolivia from Spain in 1825.
Independence Day (Jamaica), celebrates the independence of Jamaica from the United Kingdom in 1962.
